verified.refactorNotes: The candidate is a legitimate 'extract one deep committable-artifact gate' opportunity, but the original finding mis-scopes it in three ways and undercounts the coupling. (1) Sequence the work: delete the dead streamRunSection/callStructuredStreamAttempt pair first (surgical, removes a full duplicate of the trio + the 4049 checkRequiredEvidenceClasses site), then the consolidation only has to unify 3 live builders: callStructuredAttempt (paid-media), buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput (6 answer-tool sections), and the VoC-synthesis helper at ~1010. (2) The proposed gate module must accept (a) section-specific loadBearingKinds (paidMedia/VoC) via a lookup keyed on sectionId rather than two ad-hoc ternaries — this kills the bidirectional override drift — and (b) injected env so LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT and LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED are read the same way (fixes claim 4 and removes the global-env test dance). (3) The hard part is NOT the trio itself; it is the per-section escape hatches that currently sit INSIDE buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput between the required-evidence check and the evidence-support check (DemandIntent SpyFu softening that mutates committedArtifact; VoC self-sourcing + model-authored-gap). A deep module must take these as injected post-validation hooks or it will regress the needs_review softening. Also extract the 'not probed this run' sentinel to a shared constant consumed by both required-evidence.ts:203 and competitor-ad-adapter.ts:806 (currently a brittle cross-module regex with a test pinning the literal string). Severity medium not high: today the drift is latent because each live route happens to hit the builder that owns its override; this is a maintainability/footgun fix, not an active correctness bug. Update required-evidence.test.ts and evidence-support.test.ts in the same change; do NOT touch research-worker/src/competitors (legacy copy per CLAUDE.md).

verified.refactorNotes: The finding is accurate on all three claims. Real fix shape: thread structured provenance from researchInput.onboarding.economics.provenance (and competitor seeds) into the verifier as a per-field intent signal, rather than relying on the model self-labeling prose with one of two magic strings. The plumbing partially exists (SearchableSource userProvided ref already carries an optional `field`), but the core difficulty is unchanged by structured provenance: derived operator numbers ($26.25K from 35%×$75K) still don't substring-match any stored field, so you cannot key a structured rescue purely on value-equality — you'd need either (a) the section agent to emit a structured provenance tag per claim instead of a prose marker, or (b) a numeric-derivation/range check against operator-supplied source numbers. Keep the negative invariant (structural-verifier.test.ts:184+): genuinely-inferred/undisclosed claims must NOT be rescued. Note also the upstream signal is impoverished — economics+competitor provenance is hardcoded \"user-supplied\", so the schema's 5-value enum buys nothing today; enriching it (e.g. tool-measured for SearchAPI-derived numbers) is a prerequisite for a provenance verifier that does more than user-supplied-vs-absent. Severity high (not critical): this is the documented mechanism by which ~10 real fabrications slip the truthgate per the 2026-06-09 quality audit, but it degrades trustworthiness rather than crashing the pipeline.

hiddenRisks: The 33 run-section.ts `positioning...` comparisons are NOT a clean switch/dispatch table — they are scattered guard clauses (`if (input.sectionId !== "positioningVoiceOfCustomer") return ...`), per-section ad-probe gates, VoC candidate gates, paid-media gates, and competitor-landscape ad-normalization branches threaded through an 8187-line file. The shadow provider schemas exist for a REASON tied to AI SDK v6 / DeepSeek structured-output constraints: section-agent.ts L572-595 getStructuredProviderOptions branches on isDeepSeekModel/isAnthropicModel, and the shallow `.passthrough()` schemas deliberately avoid `.min()/.max()` and deep nesting that Anthropic structured-output rejects (per CLAUDE.md AI SDK rule + learned-patterns Anthropic note). paid-media (positioningPaidMediaPlan) and VoC are repeatedly special-cased across ALL three files (different loadBearingKinds, different structuredOutputMaxTokens 16384, finishReason handling, VoiceOfCustomerSectionOutputBody secondary schema name at section-agent L1371). verified.refactorNotes: The finding's direction (collapse 3 string-matched representations into one descriptor) is sound and the coupling is real and verified — but it materially undercounts the work. (1) The count is 62 identity comparisons, not ~52, and run-section.ts is 33 not 32. (2) Crucially, the run-section.ts branches are NOT pure descriptor lookups — a large share encode section-specific behavior (VoC retrieval/candidate gating, competitor ad-wall normalization, paid-media finishReason/decode handling). Those cannot be expressed as a static descriptor field; they would have to become per-section behavior hooks/strategy functions hung off the descriptor, which is a deeper change. (3) The shadow provider schemas' looseness is INTENTIONAL (provider structured-output constraints + the canonical schema still re-validates strictly at persistence), so the fix is 'derive the provider schema from the canonical one via a single shallow-projection transform' (kill the hand-maintained drift), NOT 'use the canonical schema directly' — the latter would resurrect Anthropic schema-rejection bugs. (4) A genuinely 'deep' descriptor would carry: sectionOutputSchemaName, the canonical sectionOutputSchema (already present), a derived providerBodySchema, promptMinimumGuidance text, and loadBearingKinds — and would let build-prompts/section-agent/evidence-support read fields instead of string-matching. Recommend scoping as a multi-step refactor with the order-sensitive section-registry.test.ts contract + build-prompts.test.ts + 5 run-section tests run on every step. Real severity = medium: it is a real navigability/drift hazard (a new section requires edits in 4 files in 3 string forms, and the shadow schemas can silently diverge), but it is not causing a live bug and the strict re-validation at persistence backstops the loose provider schemas.

verified.refactorNotes: The candidate is real but the framing is partly off. The 6 positioning sections are actually produced by src/lib/lab-engine/artifacts/schemas/*.ts (buyer-icp, competitor-landscape, demand-intent, market-category, offer-diagnostic, voice-of-customer + paid-media-plan/strategic-insight) using positioning* names. competitor-intel.ts also carries app-only fields beyond the 3 named (e.g. positioningMoves, clientAdInsight blocks visible at competitor-intel.ts:150+) — the drift is wider than the 4 fields cited, so a 'reconcile the schemas' task is larger in scope than the finding implies.
verified.refactorNotes: Reframe the candidate. The finding is technically accurate on duplication + drift, but assumes both copies are live, equal contracts. Reality: (1) the worker contracts.ts section schemas are dead/legacy in the live path (per-section worker execution was moved in-process to lab-engine; only corpus/identity/meeting runners survive); (2) the app journey schemas are a legacy validation/coercion layer, not the producer; (3) the real producers are src/lib/lab-engine/artifacts/schemas/* with positioning* names. So the highest-value, lowest-risk move is most likely DELETION of the dead worker section-schema half (contracts.ts section schemas + runner-cascade + runners/base + competitors/index legacy path) after confirming nothing live calls finalizeRunnerResult for the 7 sections — NOT standing up a cross-process shared package, which the Railway boundary makes expensive. If a shared package is still wanted, it should unify the lab-engine canonical schemas with the app journey schemas first (the two that are actually live), and the worker boundary can be left alone. Severity is LOW because the duplicated worker code is not on the live execution path; the only real live risk is the app-vs-lab-engine coercion layer, which this finding did not even identify. Do NOT touch research-worker/src/competitors/ (CLAUDE.md forbids it). Capture the worker build baseline before any worker-side edit (known pre-existing @types errors).

The three corpus-key knowledge sources are DELIBERATELY decoupled by a process boundary, not by accident: prefill+corpus-to-input are in src/lib/ (Vercel in-process), parse-context is in research-worker/ (separate Railway process, separate package). The non-answer normalization gap (claim 3) is the actual user-facing bug worth fixing and is independent of the descriptor refactor — it's a 5-line change (apply isNonAnswer in prefill str()), already half-mitigated by the 2026-06-09 idk-poisoning fix + the synthetic-gap-group competitor fix (32cc78c4). Bundling it into a deep-module rewrite would over-engineer a localized fix.
verified.refactorNotes: The candidate's framing ('kill sync across 6 places' by making the onboarding field a deep module) is mostly NOT a real problem at the proposed scope. The 'sync across 6 places' gotcha is real but three of those places already live inside field-catalog.ts, and the remaining three (Supabase migration, parse-context labels, identity-card JSONB) cannot be unified because they sit behind a hard process boundary (research-worker is a separate package that cannot import src/lib/) — and parse-context is legacy code the live pipeline no longer calls. A unified descriptor module is therefore either impossible (worker boundary) or speculative config-as-data over genuinely different transforms (prefill renames vs ResearchInput shape vs free-text label parsing). The one genuinely actionable, low-risk item is claim 3: apply isNonAnswer/asString-equivalent normalization in prefill-from-corpus.ts str() so the form does not display corpus non-answers ('none'/'idk') that the ad probe correctly drops to null. That is a surgical ~5-line fix, NOT a deep-module refactor. Recommend: do the claim-3 normalization fix; reject the 'descriptor deep module' consolidation as mis-scoped given the process boundary and the legacy status of parse-context.

claims.correctedReference: Add a 4th module to the smear: the committed-side freshness actually lives in src/app/api/research-v2/audit-state/route.ts (the route, not use-audit-state.ts) — line 592. use-audit-state.ts only owns the *polling cadence* + paid-media re-dispatch, not the artifact freshness rule.

claims.claim: The partial->committed handoff is an implicit rendering branch in audit-reader-shell.tsx (selects sectionPartials vs live.sectionsByZone based on activeStatus), not an explicit protocol; a final-flush partial can be silently discarded.
claims.evidence: audit-reader-shell.tsx:1080-1091 `activeDraftArtifact` returns null unless `activeStatus !== 'running'` (reads `sectionPartials[active].snapshot`); the render switches branch on activeStatus: :1344 `activeStatus === 'complete' && activeTyped` renders the committed `activeTyped` (from typedByZone←live.sectionsByZone), :1413 `activeStatus === 'running'` renders `activeDraftArtifact` (the partial). No explicit reconcile: `statusOf` :1034 flips to 'complete' the instant the polled DB row is complete, so the partial view is abandoned regardless of its seq. run-section.ts:5677 `await partialBroadcaster.flush()` does emit a final partial, but on the catch path :5715 `partialBroadcaster.cancel()` drops `pendingSnapshot` (broadcaster :118-121) — a failed attempt's last enqueued partial is silently discarded, and on success the final partial is superseded by the DB-committed artifact via a status flip, never reconciled by seq.
claims.correctedReference: The 'silent discard' is two-sided: (a) cancel() on the catch path in run-section.ts:5715 drops pendingSnapshot; (b) on the happy path the committed handoff is a status-driven branch swap (audit-reader-shell.tsx:1344 vs :1413), not a seq-reconciled merge — the last partial is simply orphaned in sectionPartials state (never cleared on commit; only cleared on runId change at use-section-partials.ts:45).

hiddenRisks: The finding's file list OMITS the two modules that actually own half the behavior: (1) src/app/api/research-v2/audit-state/route.ts:592 is where committed-side freshness (status==='complete') lives — NOT in use-audit-state.ts as implied; (2) src/lib/lab-engine/agents/run-section.ts:5581-5715 is the SOLE producer/lifecycle owner of the broadcaster (enqueue/flush/cancel). Any 'unify freshness' refactor must touch both or it changes nothing.

The two transports are fundamentally different and CANNOT be trivially unified: partials ride Supabase realtime broadcast (server→client push, ephemeral, seq-ordered) while committed artifacts ride DB rows + 2.5s polling (use-audit-state.ts:16 POLL_MS). seq is meaningless across them because the committed body is a fresh server-built object (run-section buildOutputFromStructuredBody), not the last partial snapshot. A 'single owner' would have to bridge realtime + REST polling.

seqRef is SHARED across repair-loop attempts via partialSeqRef (run-section.ts:6489 `{current:0}` threaded into every attempt at :5585/:6525/:6587/:6713). The broadcaster test pins this exact behavior — section-partial-broadcaster.test.ts:105 'continues sequence numbers from a shared repair-loop ref' asserts seq=[1,2] across two attempts. Resetting/refactoring the seq lifecycle silently breaks the repair-loop monotonicity guarantee and this test.

enqueue() has a non-obvious 'new top-level key' fast-path (section-partial-broadcaster.ts:128-141) that bypasses the throttle whenever a previously-unseen top-level key appears. Any refactor of the throttle/freshness logic must preserve this or partials will appear to stall during early streaming when the object is growing key-by-key.

use-section-partials.ts clears partial state ONLY on runId change (:45 setPartials({})) — never on section commit. So a stale partial lingers in client state after commit; it's harmless ONLY because the render branch keys on activeStatus (audit-reader-shell.tsx:1080). If a refactor changed the render to ever read sectionPartials when activeStatus==='complete', the stale partial would leak. This invariant (status-branch is the guard, not state-clearing) is implicit and undocumented.

sectionPartialPayloadSchema is .strict() (use-section-partials.ts:15) and validates seq as z.number().int().nonnegative() (:12). The broadcaster's payload shape (makeSectionPartialPayload, section-partial-broadcaster.ts:41) must stay byte-compatible with this strict client schema across the wire or every partial is silently dropped at use-section-partials.ts:65. This producer/consumer contract spans two files with no shared type enforcement at the broadcast boundary (realtime is untyped JSON).

broadcastSectionPartial requires SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (realtime-broadcast.ts:32) on the server and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY on the client (use-section-partials.ts:48). Partials degrade to silent no-op (onError warns, run-section.ts:5586-5592) when env is missing — meaning in many envs the 'partial' path is dead and only the committed poll runs. A refactor assuming partials are always live would regress envs where they're disabled.

realtime-broadcast.ts is bidirectionally coupled to section-partial-broadcaster.ts (imports makeSectionPartialPayload + SectionPartialPublishInput at :1-2; broadcaster imports broadcastSectionPartial back at :1). This circular-ish import pair means a 'single owner' module split has to break or relocate makeSectionPartialPayload, touching both files' import graph.
verified.refactorNotes: Both claims are factually CONFIRMED, but realSeverity is LOW, not a structural defect worth a risky refactor. Reasons: (1) The 'smear' is the natural consequence of two genuinely different transports (Supabase realtime push for live partials vs DB-row REST polling for the durable committed artifact) — they cannot share a seq because the committed body is a freshly server-assembled object, not the final partial. seq is correctly scoped to the realtime transport where ordering can actually invert. (2) The 'silent discard' of a final-flush partial is BY DESIGN and harmless: on success the committed DB artifact (richer, verified, typed) supersedes the last partial via a status-driven branch swap; on failure cancel() drops a partial that would be wrong to show anyway (the attempt errored). No user-visible bug results — the worst case is a sub-2.5s window where a section shows the last partial before the poll flips it to committed, which is the intended progressive-disclosure UX. If a refactor IS pursued, the cleanest framing is NOT 'one owner' but 'make the producer/consumer contract explicit': (a) lift the partial payload type into a shared schema enforced at both broadcast and subscribe boundaries (today only the client .strict()-validates); (b) clear sectionPartials[zone] on commit in use-section-partials so stale partials don't linger (currently relies on the activeStatus render-branch as the implicit guard); (c) document the seqRef sharing-across-repair-attempts invariant. Do NOT attempt to merge the realtime and polling freshness into one mechanism — that's a far larger change (would need realtime for committed rows too) for no correctness gain. Mandatory: update section-partial-broadcaster.test.ts (pins seq=[1,2] across attempts, throttle coalescing, flush-after-failure) and run it in any targeted test run — the parity tests will NOT catch a broadcaster behavior change.

claims.claim: saveArtifact has a tiny signature but hides an ~8-step fan-out: agentic review (blocking LLM ~45s) -> commitArtifactSection (DB CAS + rollup trigger) -> telemetry -> claimProfilePersist -> isParentAuditComplete -> profile persist / quality gate / shared snapshot, in that order
claims.evidence: run-store.ts:34-37 declares `saveArtifact: (runId: string, artifact: ArtifactEnvelope) => Promise<RunRecord>` (tiny signature). supabase-run-store.ts impl runs in order: L897 `attachAgenticReview({...timeoutMs: reviewTimeoutMs})` (blocking; L160 `defaultLabReviewTimeoutMs = 45_000`; calls real LLM `reviewAndUpgradeSection` w/ `reviewModel`); L912 `adapter.commitArtifactSection({...})`; L942-961 `.from('research_section_runs').update({telemetry:...})`; L966 `claimProfilePersist`; L973 `isParentAuditComplete`; L976+ inside `if (parentAuditComplete)`: L979 `persistAuditProfileBestEffort`, L994 `markProfileSynced`, L999 `emitProfilePersistedEvent`, L1006 `refreshV3SharedSessionSnapshotsBestEffort`, L1012 `persistLiveQualityGateBestEffort`; else-branch L1023 `patchProfileSectionBestEffort`.
claims.correctedReference: Step order/exact-count caveat: it is one CAS commit + telemetry + a 2-step completion check (claimProfilePersist then isParentAuditComplete) + a 4-call completion fan-out (persist profile, sync/emit event, share snapshot, quality gate) OR a single best-effort section profile patch on the non-completing path. The 'rollup trigger' is not a DB trigger — it is a synchronous `perform public.roll_up_research_artifact(p_artifact_id)` call inside the commit_artifact_section RPC (supabase/migrations/20260604_...sql:100-101,144-145). saveArtifact body spans supabase-run-store.ts:872-1040.

claims.claim: Read-after-write dependency on the DB rollup trigger: code reads research_artifacts.status right after the commit that triggers roll_up_research_artifact; correct only because Postgres is synchronous in-txn; invisible in the RunStore interface
claims.evidence: commit_artifact_section RPC ends with `if p_patch->>'status' = 'complete' then perform public.roll_up_research_artifact(p_artifact_id); end if;` (20260604_...sql:100-101 and 144-145) which does `update public.research_artifacts a set ... status = case when a.children_total > 0 and v_complete >= a.children_total then 'complete' else a.status end` (20260603_...sql:154-162). Immediately after `adapter.commitArtifactSection` returns (supabase-run-store.ts:912), the code reads that same status: claimProfilePersist L966 does `.from('research_artifacts').update({profile_persisted_at}).eq('id',...).eq('status','complete').is('profile_persisted_at',null)` (def L685-707), and isParentAuditComplete L973 does `.from('research_artifacts').select('status')...status === 'complete'` (def L709-726). Both depend on the rollup having already flipped status to 'complete' synchronously within the RPC. The RunStore interface (run-store.ts:34-37) exposes none of this.
claims.correctedReference: Precise mechanism: roll_up runs synchronously inside the same RPC/transaction as the section commit, so when commit_artifact_section returns ok, research_artifacts.status is already updated and the subsequent SELECT/UPDATE sees it. It is a same-RPC ordering dependency (commit RPC internally fans into rollup), not strictly a separate-statement read-after-write across the app boundary — the app-level read just confirms what the RPC already committed.

hiddenRisks: TWO RunStore implementations share one interface: supabase-run-store.ts (full fan-out, L872) and the file-based run-store.ts (L498, NO fan-out — just file write). A refactor that makes the side effects 'explicit' in the signature/interface would force the file impl to satisfy a contract it does not need, or break interface parity. The current design deliberately hides the fan-out behind a uniform tiny signature so both impls are swappable.

Four production callers, all in src/lib/lab-engine/agents/run-section.ts (L1490, L7115, L7558, L8153) call `deps.store.saveArtifact(input.runId, artifact)` and rely on it being the single 'commit + everything' entry point. Splitting the fan-out into separate calls would require updating every section-runner call site and risks ordering bugs (e.g. profile persist before commit).

Heavy test coupling: supabase-run-store.test.ts (1135 lines) mocks each fan-out step independently (reviewAndUpgradeSection L325, persistAuditProfileBestEffort L323, refreshV3SharedSessionSnapshotsBestEffort L333) and asserts exact call order/counts, the profile_persisted event (L489), and a two-store CAS race proving exactly-once profile persist (L772-784). Any extraction must preserve these mock seams or the suite breaks.

Exactly-once / CAS invariant: claimProfilePersist relies on the atomic conditional UPDATE (`.eq('status','complete').is('profile_persisted_at',null)`) to win the race among the (up to 6) sibling sections that may finish near-simultaneously. The fan-out ordering (claim BEFORE isParentAuditComplete, and profilePersistClaimed short-circuiting the status read) is load-bearing for not double-persisting the profile or double-emitting profile_persisted. A naive 'make it explicit' refactor that reorders these would reintroduce the double-persist bug the CAS was added to fix.

capstone/paid-media degrade-to-needs-review branch (L905-909, isCapstoneSection + input.evidenceCoverage?.ready === false) is woven INTO the commit-patch build, not the fan-out — easy to drop when extracting steps.

The agentic review is fail-soft: attachAgenticReview catches all errors and commits the ORIGINAL artifact (L591-599 try/catch returning input.artifact). Making the LLM step 'explicit/separate' risks turning a best-effort upgrade into a hard dependency if the catch is lost.

commit_artifact_section RPC also dual-writes research_section_runs status (migration L93-99, 130-136) and the rollup re-checks counts_toward_rollup=true sections only (20260603 L146-151) — the app-level telemetry update (L942) and the in-RPC section-run status write both touch research_section_runs, a second non-obvious write-coupling the finding did not mention.
verified.refactorNotes: This is a code-readability/altitude finding, not a bug — the behavior is correct and well-tested. Severity is LOW: the 'hidden' fan-out is intentional encapsulation behind a stable RunStore port (two impls, swappable), and the read-after-write is safe by construction (the rollup runs synchronously inside the same commit_artifact_section RPC, so the post-commit status reads always observe the rolled-up status). If pursuing 'make the fan-out explicit', do it by extracting named private steps WITHIN supabase-run-store.ts (e.g. a `runPostCommitProfileFanout(...)` helper) rather than widening the RunStore interface — widening the port would leak Supabase-specific concerns into the file-based impl and break parity. Keep the existing mock seams (the three vi.fn'd helpers) so supabase-run-store.test.ts keeps passing, and preserve the claimProfilePersist-then-isParentAuditComplete order plus the CAS exactly-once race test. Do NOT 'simplify' the same-RPC rollup dependency into an explicit app-side re-query loop; that would add a round-trip and a race window the current design avoids. The finding slightly mischaracterizes 'DB rollup trigger' — it is a synchronous SQL function call inside the commit RPC, not a row trigger; verify any doc/comment uses that precise language."

graph.notes:
SEQUENCING RATIONALE. The hard constraint from the brief — c1/c2/c3 each carve a piece out of c4, so c4 is LAST among those four — is honored: Phase 1 (dead-code deletion) -> Phase 2 (c1 gate) -> Phase 3 (c2 provenance) -> Phase 4 (c3 descriptor) -> Phase 5 (c4 decomposition). Phase 1 is split out as its own shippable unit because the verified findings flag a DEAD-PATH TRAP: extracting a shared validation helper BEFORE deleting streamRunSection/callStructuredStreamAttempt would wire dead code into the live path and make it look live. Deleting first drops 744 lines AND one of the 3 validation copies for free, so c1 only has to unify 2 live builders.

WHY c2 BEFORE c3, BOTH AFTER c1: c1 establishes the sectionId->loadBearingKinds lookup and injected-env pattern; c2's provenance read and c3's descriptor fields are the natural consumers. Landing c3 first would force re-touching the gate and verifier. c2 and c3 both depend on Phase 1 (clean file) and c2 specifically benefits from c1's consolidated gate (evidence-support is where the rescue/shortfall logic lives).

TWO CLAIMS WERE REFUTED/RESCOPED in the findings and the plan respects them: (c5 claim-3) the rollup SQL ALREADY filters counts_toward_rollup=true (20260603:151) — DROP any 'add the filter' work; (c6) the worker section schemas are DEAD, so the move is DELETION not a shared package; (c7) the deep-descriptor consolidation is mis-scoped (worker boundary + legacy parse-context) — ship only the ~5-line prefill normalization. These rescopes turn three would-be 'high-effort' phases into low/medium-risk surgical units.

ADR COMPLIANCE: No phase introduces a per-brick artifact-builder tool or generic envelope (ADR-0002 — every section stays one structured output; c1/c3 consolidate the GATE/DESCRIPTOR around that single output, they do not fragment it). No phase reintroduces an evidence-ledger store, threaded dossier with awaited waves, or orchestrator control-plane (ADR-0010 — c2 annotates the EXISTING ResearchInput.onboarding.provenance; c5 keeps orchestrate awaiting only an ACK with section work in Vercel after()). The worker-cannot-import-src/lib boundary is why c6 is delete-not-share and c7 rejects the unified descriptor.

LIVE E2E DISCIPLINE: Per the brief, live E2E gates ONLY phases that touch the run path. Gated: 2 (c1), 3 (c2), 4 (c3), 5 (c4), 6 (c5 dispatch), 9 (c9 commit/persist). NOT gated (build+targeted tests suffice): 1 (dead code), 7 (dead worker code), 8 (display normalization), c8 portion of 9 (display contract). Each E2E uses the existing scripts/zz-verify-e2e-run.mjs gate (~$2 DeepSeek/Airtable run) — re-runnable, not a one-off.

TEST-COUPLING WARNINGS BAKED INTO GATES: section-registry.test.ts is order-sensitive on allowedTools and is the ONLY test that catches an allowedTools change (Phase 4). required-evidence.test.ts pins the not-probed sentinel literal + does an env-mutation dance that Phase 2 must rewrite to injected env. run-lab-section route.test.ts pins the exact autonomy-gate accept condition (Phase 6). supabase-run-store.test.ts pins the CAS exactly-once race and 3 mock seams (Phase 9). section-partial-broadcaster.test.ts pins seq=[1,2] across repair attempts (Phase 9/c8). Worker build baseline must be captured BEFORE Phase 7 (known pre-existing @types errors).

PROD AUTO-DEPLOY CAUTION: main pushes auto-deploy to Vercel. Each phase is independently shippable, so land them as separate commits/PRs and let each clear its own gate before the next merges — do NOT batch the high-risk lab-engine phases (2-5) into one push. Branch off main per phase.

SEVERITY-WEIGHTED VALUE: c2 (high) is the only phase that closes a real client-trust defect (the ~10 fabrications from the 2026-06-09 quality audit). c4 (high) is the biggest maintainability win but pure refactor. c1/c3 (medium) are footgun/drift fixes, latent not active. c5 (medium) is latent UX-drift. c6/c7/c8/c9 (low) are cleanup. If forced to ship a subset, prioritize 1->2->3 (gate consolidation + the actual fabrication fix), defer 4/5 (god-module split) and the entire low-risk tail."

design.moduleName: committable-gate (src/lib/lab-engine/sections/committable-gate.ts) — a single deep module that answers "is this Section Artifact committable, and if not, why and how does it degrade?" for one attempt. Renders the validateMinimums → checkRequiredEvidenceClasses → (section escape hatches) → evaluateEvidenceSupport pipeline as ONE call: evaluateCommittableAttempt(...). The cross-module ad-evidence not-probed sentinel string moves to a shared constant in this module (or a sibling sentinels.ts it owns), consumed by both required-evidence.ts and competitor-ad-adapter.ts.

interface.whatCallersMustKnow: ONE function: evaluateCommittableAttempt(args) -> CommittableVerdict, used by the two LIVE attempt builders (callStructuredAttempt for paid-media; buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput for the 6 answer-tool sections). Callers no longer hand-sequence the trio or hand-pick loadBearingKinds. They pass: the candidate artifact, the SectionDefinition (carries validateMinimums + requiredEvidenceClasses + sectionId), the VerificationReport, an injected env (deps.env ?? process.env), and an injected per-section postRequiredEvidenceHook (the escape hatches). The module owns: (a) the FIXED canonical order minimums -> requiredEvidence -> postRequiredEvidenceHook -> evidenceSupport; (b) the sectionId->loadBearingKinds lookup (paidMedia=['url'], VoC=['numeric','url','quote'], else default ['numeric','url']) replacing the two ad-hoc ternaries; (c) reading LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT from the INJECTED env (not global process.env). The separate orchestrator-side gate getEvidenceGateFailureReason(attempt, maxUnsupportedAllowed) is EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE for phase 1 — it operates on AttemptResult ceilings (LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED via getMaxUnsupportedAllowed, default Infinity) and is the ADR-0010 advisory ungate; we keep it where it is, only feeding it the verdict's unsupportedLoadBearing count. Callers must still build the AttemptResult shape from the verdict (the module returns the parts; it does NOT construct the section-specific gap artifacts or AttemptResult).
interface.inputs: args: { artifact: ArtifactEnvelope (the candidate, post-normalization — see invariants); definition: SectionDefinition; verification: VerificationReport; env: Record<string,string|undefined>; postRequiredEvidenceHook?: (ctx:{artifact,verification,definition,sectionId,researchInput,deps,modelSteps}) => HookOutcome }. HookOutcome is a tagged union: {kind:'ok'} | {kind:'reject', errors:string[], gapArtifact?: ArtifactEnvelope} | {kind:'soften', artifact: ArtifactEnvelope}. The hook is where VoC self-sourcing + model-authored-gap (reject) and DemandIntent SpyFu softening (soften, mutating which artifact gets committed) live — injected by the caller, NOT baked into the gate, so per-section behavior stays at the section boundary.
interface.output: CommittableVerdict tagged union: {kind:'minimumsFailed', errors:string[]} | {kind:'requiredEvidenceMissing', missingClass:RequiredEvidenceClass, unsupportedCount:number, verifiedCount:number} | {kind:'hookReject', errors:string[], gapArtifact?:ArtifactEnvelope} | {kind:'evidenceShortfall', committableArtifact:ArtifactEnvelope, shortfall:EvidenceSupportShortfall} | {kind:'committable', committableArtifact:ArtifactEnvelope}. The committableArtifact carried in evidenceShortfall/committable is the post-hook artifact (the softened variant when the hook returned 'soften'), so the ADR-0010 advisory-commit path downstream commits the softened body. The caller maps the verdict to its existing AttemptResult fields (requiredEvidenceMissing -> RequiredEvidenceMissingError, evidenceShortfall -> evidenceSupportShortfall, hookReject.gapArtifact -> voiceOfCustomerEvidenceGapArtifact etc.).
interface.errorModes: Pure/synchronous; does NOT throw for gate failures — every failure is a verdict variant (mirrors today's null-artifact returns, NOT today's RequiredEvidenceMissingError throw; the Error object is reconstructed by the caller from the requiredEvidenceMissing verdict to preserve recordSectionFailure / counts_toward_rollup wiring). The ONLY throw path it must preserve is the upstream one it depends on but does not own: checkRequiredEvidenceClasses->hasAdEvidenceOrGap iterates advertiserGroups and an empty [] is a hard RequiredEvidenceMissingError unless normalization injected a synthetic gap group first (see invariants). The injected postRequiredEvidenceHook is trusted to be pure-ish; any throw inside it propagates to the caller unchanged.
interface.invariants: (1) ORDER IS FIXED AND SINGLE-SOURCE: minimums -> requiredEvidence -> hook -> evidenceSupport. This deliberately unifies the two live builders that today disagree (callStructuredAttempt computes evidenceSupport BEFORE minimums/required at run-section.ts:3765 then 3778/3811; buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput computes it AFTER at 5363). The module picks buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput's order (evidence-support last, after the hook can soften) as canonical — verify paid-media still gates identically under this order (it should: evidenceSupport is read-only over verification and order-independent of minimums for the shortfall result). (2) loadBearingKinds is keyed on sectionId via ONE lookup, killing the bidirectional drift (VoC override present only at 5363, paid-media only at 3765 today). (3) LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT is read from injected env, same discipline as getMaxUnsupportedAllowed — removing the global-process.env read at required-evidence.ts:229. (4) The gate ASSUMES competitor ad-evidence normalization already ran upstream (withNormalizedCompetitorAdEvidence at run-section.ts:1982 via withNormalizedSectionOutput, injecting buildEmptyCompetitorAdEvidenceGapGroup when the wall is []); the gate must NEVER see an empty advertiserGroups wall or hasAdEvidenceOrGap hard-throws (prod regression 0eeebd93). Phase 1 keeps normalization where it is — it is an input precondition, not part of the gate. (5) not-probed sentinel is now ONE exported constant shared by producer (competitor-ad-adapter.ts:806) and matcher (required-evidence.ts:203), no longer a duplicated free-text regex.

behindSeam: The fixed canonical sequencing of validateMinimums -> checkRequiredEvidenceClasses -> postRequiredEvidenceHook -> evaluateEvidenceSupport (today re-stated by hand in callStructuredAttempt and buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput, in two DIFFERENT orders)

The sectionId -> loadBearingKinds resolution (paidMediaLoadBearingKinds / voiceOfCustomerLoadBearingKinds / default) — replaces the two divergent inline ternaries at run-section.ts:3765 and 5363, eliminating the bidirectional override drift

Construction of the RequiredEvidenceMissing verdict payload (missingClass + unsupportedCount + verifiedCount) so all paths surface the missing class identically (today built at 3818 and 5261 but NOT at the VoC-synthesis helper 1010)
Injected env read of LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT (the value still consumed inside hasAdEvidenceOrGap, but threaded through checkRequiredEvidenceClasses' input instead of a global process.env read)
The shared NOT_PROBED_SENTINEL constant (string + matcher) — moved out of the brittle cross-module regex into one exported symbol consumed by both required-evidence.ts and competitor-ad-adapter.ts
callSitesCollapsed: src/lib/lab-engine/sections/required-evidence.ts:229 (global process.env.LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT read -> becomes injected env param)
src/lib/lab-engine/sections/required-evidence.ts:203 + src/lib/lab-engine/agents/tools/competitor-ad-adapter.ts:806 (duplicated not-probed sentinel regex/string -> one shared constant)

NOT collapsed (out of scope, left in place): run-section.ts:1010 (VoC-synthesis helper, minimums+required only — see migration step for whether it is dead), run-section.ts:4016/4049 (callStructuredStreamAttempt, DEAD — deleted in step 1, not migrated), run-section.ts:6238/6817/7943/8072 (getEvidenceGateFailureReason orchestrator ceiling gate — distinct concern, ADR-0010 advisory ungate)
testsSurvive: NEW: committable-gate.test.ts unit-tests the whole verdict matrix directly — minimumsFailed / requiredEvidenceMissing(class+counts) / hookReject / evidenceShortfall / committable — with the loadBearingKinds lookup asserted per sectionId (paidMedia=url-only, VoC=+quote, default=numeric+url). This is the new seam: today these branches are only reachable through the 8000-line god-module, never unit-testable in isolation.

NEW: a test pinning that the not-probed sentinel constant is byte-identical to what competitor-ad-adapter emits and what required-evidence matches (replaces the implicit cross-module string coupling; subsumes the literal-string pin at required-evidence.test.ts:214).

CHANGES: required-evidence.test.ts:170-178,214,236 — the save/delete/restore process.env.LAB_AD_EVIDENCE_STRICT dance is replaced by passing env into checkRequiredEvidenceClasses (or the gate) as a param; the literal sentinel assertion at :214 now references the shared constant.

CHANGES: evidence-support.test.ts — getMaxUnsupportedAllowed env-injection tests (:179-196) are UNTOUCHED (that function and the orchestrator ceiling gate stay as-is); evaluateEvidenceSupport unit tests stay but the loadBearingKinds selection moves under the gate's lookup so a new test asserts the lookup rather than the caller's ternary.

UNAFFECTED but must be RE-RUN green every step: with-normalized-competitor-ad-evidence.test.ts (normalization precondition unchanged), competitor-ad-adapter.test.ts (sentinel producer), section-registry.test.ts (allowedTools/requiredEvidenceClasses contract — gate reads requiredEvidenceClasses off the definition, does not alter it), and the run-section*.test.ts behavior suites that exercise the live builders.
migrationSteps: STEP 0 (separate surgical commit, BEFORE any consolidation): delete the dead pipeline — streamRunSection (run-section.ts:7166) + streamSectionViaAnswerTool + callStructuredStreamAttempt (3878) — verified zero live callers (grep clean: only lab-section-job.ts imports runSection). This removes one whole copy of the trio (the 4049/4016 site) so the gate only has to unify the 2 LIVE builders. CAUTION from findings: StreamRunSectionDeps must NOT be deleted with it — it is still used by live attempt-context types at ~6 sites; delete only the dead functions, keep the interface. Run full lab-engine test suite green.

STEP 1: extract NOT_PROBED_SENTINEL (constant + isNotProbedSentinel matcher) into the new module (or sentinels.ts), import it in required-evidence.ts:203 and competitor-ad-adapter.ts:806. Pure move, no behavior change. Pin with the new sentinel-parity test + existing required-evidence.test.ts:214. Green.

STEP 2: thread env injection into checkRequiredEvidenceClasses (add env param, default process.env to keep all 4 — now 3 — call sites compiling unchanged), have hasAdEvidenceOrGap read the passed env. Update required-evidence.test.ts to pass env instead of mutating global. This is independent of the gate and keeps every path green. Green.

STEP 3: write committable-gate.ts (evaluateCommittableAttempt) with the verdict union, loadBearingKinds lookup, and the postRequiredEvidenceHook seam — fully unit-tested in committable-gate.test.ts. Module not yet wired into run-section. Green (new file + new tests only).

STEP 4: migrate buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput FIRST (it is the canonical order the gate adopts, so the diff is smallest). Replace its inline minimums/required/escape-hatch/evidence-support block (5214-5375) with: build the VoC + DemandIntent escape hatches as a postRequiredEvidenceHook closure, call evaluateCommittableAttempt, map verdict -> existing AttemptResult fields. Run the 6 answer-tool sections' run-section*.test.ts. Green before touching the other builder.

STEP 5: migrate callStructuredAttempt (paid-media live path). Its current order computes evidence-support first; switching to the gate's canonical (last) order must be verified to produce an identical shortfall for paid-media (evidenceSupport is a read-only fold over verification; order vs minimums does not change its result). Its hook is the VoC self-sourcing block only (no DemandIntent softening on this path today — confirm). Map verdict -> AttemptResult. Run paid-media + run-section route tests. Green.

STEP 6: investigate the VoC-synthesis helper at run-section.ts:1010 — it does minimums+required but no evidence-support and no RequiredEvidenceMissingError (returns undefined). Determine if it is live or vestigial; if live, route it through the gate (with an evidence-support-skipping flag or by treating it as a pre-check) only AFTER confirming behavior parity; if vestigial, leave or delete in a separate commit. Do NOT fold it in blind — it has a genuinely different return contract.

STEP 7: leave getEvidenceGateFailureReason (1560) and getMaxUnsupportedAllowed entirely alone — they are the ADR-0010 advisory ceiling gate at the orchestrator layer, a distinct concern from per-attempt committability. Phase 1 explicitly does not touch them; the gate just supplies the unsupportedLoadBearing list they already consume via AttemptResult.
risks: ORDER FLIP on the paid-media path: callStructuredAttempt today evaluates evidence-support BEFORE minimums/required; the unified gate evaluates it LAST. Behaviorally equivalent for the SHORTFALL result (read-only fold), but if any downstream code reads evidenceSupportShortfall on a minimums-failed attempt, the timing changes. Mitigation: step 5 asserts identical AttemptResult for a paid-media fixture before/after.

ESCAPE-HATCH MUTATION REGRESSION: the DemandIntent SpyFu softening rebinds committedArtifact between required and evidence-support; if the postRequiredEvidenceHook seam does not faithfully carry the 'soften' outcome into the committableArtifact, needs_review softening is lost and DemandIntent hard-fails (the exact W1 ungate the softening was built for). Mitigation: the HookOutcome 'soften' variant returns the replacement artifact and the gate threads it to evidence-support and the final verdict; pinned in committable-gate.test.ts.

NORMALIZATION PRECONDITION: if anyone later moves the gate earlier than withNormalizedSectionOutput (run-section.ts:2860), an empty advertiserGroups wall reaches hasAdEvidenceOrGap and hard-throws (prod 0eeebd93). Mitigation: invariant documented; gate's doc comment states normalization is an input precondition; phase 1 does not move normalization.

ENV-PARAM RIPPLE: adding env to checkRequiredEvidenceClasses changes its signature; the dead-path site is gone after step 0 but the live sites + the VoC-synthesis helper (1010) all need the param (defaulted to process.env to avoid a big-bang). Mitigation: default the param so untouched callers compile; required-evidence.test.ts updated same commit.

RequiredEvidenceMissingError RECONSTRUCTION: the gate returns a verdict, not the Error; the caller rebuilds the Error for recordSectionFailure / counts_toward_rollup / fan-out wiring (deploy-blockers 2026-06-08). If a caller forgets to map requiredEvidenceMissing -> Error, orchestration loses the failure signal. Mitigation: keep the Error construction in the (now single) mapper helper at the caller; assert recordSectionFailure receives it in the route test.

SCOPE DISCIPLINE: the orchestrator ceiling gate (getEvidenceGateFailureReason) LOOKS like a 4th copy of the gate and is tempting to fold in. It is a different altitude (ADR-0010 advisory, ceiling-based, Infinity default). Folding it in would entangle the per-attempt committability decision with the advisory ungate and risk reintroducing a hard-fail default. Explicitly excluded.
design.adrAlignment: "ADR-0002 (single output): the gate decides committability of the ONE Section Artifact per section; it does not add alternative outputs, scoring branches, or a second deliverable. The committableArtifact it returns is the same single envelope, optionally the softened (needs_review) variant — no new output shape, no revived multi-artifact/capstone pattern (those were removed in Stage B). ADR-0010 (ARI / advisory ungate, DeepSeek-only, annotate-not-replace): the module PRESERVES the advisory posture. It does NOT make the evidence-support shortfall a hard fail — evidenceShortfall is a verdict the caller can still commit-with-honest-badge from, exactly as today; the hard ceiling (LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED, default Infinity) stays in the orchestrator gate untouched, so the 'never hard-fail by default' rejected-pattern reversal is not reintroduced. LoadBearingKinds remain per-section ANNOTATIONS of which claim kinds matter (VoC quotes, paid-media URLs), consistent with ARI's annotate-the-input philosophy rather than deleting sections. The not-probed sentinel becoming a shared constant strengthens the honest-gap (coverage) signal ARI depends on, rather than masking gaps. No rejected pattern (finite-default hard gate, capstone synthesis, client-only autonomy) is reintroduced."

decisions.question: Each merged phase auto-deploys to prod (Vercel GitHub integration on main). Do we ship each refactor phase straight to main, or accumulate phases on a branch and ship as a batch behind one live E2E gate?
options: Ship each phase straight to main as it passes tsc + targeted tests (fast feedback, prod auto-deploys each time)
Accumulate all refactor phases on a feature branch, run ONE live ~$2 E2E gate at the end, then FF-merge as a batch

Hybrid: ship pure-deletion/dead-code phases (c4 dead pipelines, c6 dead worker schemas) straight to main; accumulate behavior-touching phases (c1 gate consolidation, c2 provenance verifier) on a branch behind the E2E gate
decisions.recommendation: Hybrid (option 3). These are maintainability refactors with deep test coupling, not features — but two of them (c1 gate consolidation, c2 provenance verifier) sit directly on the truthgate/evidence-support hot path that the 2026-06-09 quality audit already flagged as fragile (~10 fabrications slipping). Pure deletions are revert-trivial and tests prove zero dangling refs, so they don't warrant holding a branch. Behavior-touching gate/verifier work does — it should not auto-deploy to prod without the live E2E gate (now re-runnable via scripts/zz-verify-e2e-run.mjs) confirming sections still commit.
decisions.whyItNeedsUser: This is a risk-appetite / cost call, not a technical default. The live E2E gate costs ~$2 and time; the user has previously chosen to ship WITHOUT live E2E (deploy-blockers 2026-06-08) and also to gate strictly (Stage B). Auto-deploy-on-main means the cadence choice directly controls prod blast radius, and only the user knows how much they want to spend on gates vs. velocity right now.

decisions.question: c2 provenance verifier: extend structured per-field Provenance to ALL onboarding fields now, or only the load-bearing economics + competitor-seed set that already has provenance written?
options: Only economics + competitor seeds (the two paths that already write provenance) — make the verifier READ the existing structured provenance instead of the 2-magic-string prose markers
Extend provenance writing to all onboarding fields (media-plan fields, ICP, etc.) in corpus-to-research-input.ts first, THEN make the verifier consume it broadly
Enrich the provenance ENUM too (tool-measured / source-reported vs the current hardcoded "user-supplied") so the verifier can distinguish SearchAPI-derived numbers from operator-stated ones
decisions.recommendation: Option 1, scoped tight. The finding is explicit that the hard part is unchanged by going broad: derived operator numbers ($26.25K from 35%x$75K) never substring-match any stored field, which is the exact reason the prose-marker hack exists. The real fix is either (a) the section agent emits a structured per-claim provenance tag, or (b) a numeric-derivation check against operator source numbers — both scoped to economics first. The schema's 5-value enum buys nothing today (everything is hardcoded "user-supplied"), so option 3 is a separate prerequisite, not this slice. Going broad (option 2) is speculative config-as-data the CLAUDE.md simplicity rule warns against — most onboarding fields don't produce the fabrication class the audit caught.
decisions.whyItNeedsUser: The audit found the fabrications cluster in BuyerICP (invented audience numbers) and DemandIntent (82 comments/85K) too, not just economics — so 'only economics' might leave known fabrications unaddressed. Whether to chase those now (broader scope) or ship the economics rescue and treat ICP/DemandIntent fabrications as a follow-up is a product-trust judgment about what 'client-trustworthy' means this week. That's the user's bar to set, not a default.

decisions.question: c5 (dispatch authority — 4 completeness predicates + duplicated route helpers) is in a different part of the tree (API routes / SQL / client hooks) than c1/c3/c4 (lab-engine internals). Run c5 in a parallel second session, or serialize it after the lab-engine work?
options: Run c5 in a parallel session now — it touches API routes + use-audit-state + audit-reader-shell, near-disjoint from the lab-engine run-section.ts cluster
Serialize c5 after c4 — both touch the autonomy/fan-out wiring that route.test.ts pins, and the deploy-blockers history shows this area bites

Drop c5's completeness-predicate unification entirely; keep only the cheap helper extractions (buildLabSectionProviderPreflightResponse x3, buildCommittedArtifactsResearchInput x2) and leave the 4 predicates alone
decisions.recommendation: Option 3 for the risky half, parallel-safe for the cheap half. The helper extractions are mechanical and genuinely disjoint from lab-engine — safe to parallelize. But unifying the 4 completeness predicates is the deceptively dangerous part: the finding confirms they read three different data shapes and deriveParentStatus is INTENTIONALLY stricter (all 6 worker rows present, no fallback). The worker-OR-artifact fallback exists for a real race (worker 'complete' vs artifact projection). Collapsing them risks regressing the autonomy invariant the deploy-blockers fix (2026-06-08) just stabilized. Also note claim 3 (no SQL rollup filter) was REFUTED — drop that work item entirely.
decisions.whyItNeedsUser: Parallelizing is a throughput-vs-coordination tradeoff only the user can price against their attention budget (ADHD context: more sessions = more context-switching). And whether to touch the completeness predicates AT ALL is a 'is latent drift worth disturbing recently-stabilized autonomy wiring' call — the area has burned the project before, so the appetite for poking it is the user's to set.

decisions.question: How aggressive on deleting dead code in run-section.ts and the worker? c4 confirms ~744 dead lines (streamRunSection + streamSectionViaAnswerTool, zero prod callers); c6 confirms a whole dead worker section-schema half. But StreamRunSectionDeps must SURVIVE the run-section deletion (still used by 6 live sites), and the worker has the CLAUDE.md-forbidden competitors/ dir nearby.
options: Aggressive: delete the 744 dead lines in run-section.ts AND the dead worker section-schema half (contracts.ts schemas + runner-cascade + runners/base) in one sweep
Conservative: delete only the 744 dead lines in run-section.ts now (cleanest, zero-caller-verified, and it removes one of the 3 duplicate validation copies for free); defer the worker deletion

Surgical-staged: delete run-section dead pipelines first as its own commit (sequencing requirement for c1/c4 anyway), then evaluate the worker deletion separately after confirming nothing live calls finalizeRunnerResult for the 7 sections
decisions.recommendation: Option 3 (surgical-staged), which the findings independently demand. Deleting streamRunSection/streamSectionViaAnswerTool FIRST is a hard sequencing requirement: it cuts the c1 gate consolidation from 4 call sites to 3 (removes the dead callStructuredStreamAttempt validation copy) and must happen BEFORE any shared-helper extraction, or you wire dead code into a live path. Keep StreamRunSectionDeps (load-bearing). The worker deletion (c6) is lower-risk than building a shared package but needs its own verification that finalizeRunnerResult is truly dead for the 7 sections, plus capturing the worker's pre-existing @types build baseline first — so it's a separate decision, not a blanket sweep.
decisions.whyItNeedsUser: The worker half is the question with real taste: c6 reframes the whole candidate as 'delete the dead worker copy' rather than 'build a shared package,' but it sits one directory from competitors/ which CLAUDE.md forbids touching, and the worker build has its own broken baseline. Whether to open the worker at all this cycle — versus leaving a known-dead-but-quiet copy alone — is a 'don't poke the separate Railway process' judgment the user should make.

decisions.question: c6 worker contract drift (pricingSourceUrl / competitorSources / sourceConfidence enum-vs-string): pin the seam with a cheap golden-fixture test, do a full codegen step, or skip the seam entirely because the worker copy is dead?
options: Golden-fixture / cross-validation test (cheap): a single test that asserts the worker and app schema key-sets + drifted fields match, failing loudly on future drift
Codegen step (more work): generate the worker contracts.ts from a single source so they cannot drift — requires a new build step across the Railway process boundary
Skip the seam entirely: the finding shows the worker section schemas are dead/legacy (not on the live path), so just DELETE the dead worker half instead of enforcing parity
decisions.recommendation: Option 3, falling back to nothing. c6 is the lowest-severity candidate (rated LOW) precisely because the worker section schemas are no longer the live producer — the 6 positioning sections run in-process via lab-engine, and the worker /run only dispatches corpus/identity/meeting. Enforcing a seam (golden test OR codegen) on a dead copy is wasted effort; codegen across the hard Railway boundary is expensive speculative infra. If anything is worth a golden test, it's the app-vs-lab-engine coercion layer (LEGACY_TO_CANONICAL_SECTION_MAP) the finding says was the REAL live risk nobody scoped — but that's a different artifact than what c6 named.
decisions.whyItNeedsUser: Cheap-test-vs-codegen-vs-delete is a classic effort/durability tradeoff, and the twist is that the 'correct' answer per the skeptic pass is 'the premise is stale — delete instead of enforce.' Confirming that the worker section path is truly dead before deleting (vs. keeping a cheap guard test as insurance) is a risk call about a separate deployed process the user owns operationally.

gateDesignHoles: FALSE PREMISE — callStructuredAttempt is NOT 'paid-media' (design says 'callStructuredAttempt @3706 = paid-media'). Verified in run-section.ts: the LIVE callStructuredAttempt call sites are 7780/7829/7901/8034, all INSIDE runSection (the canonical live entry at 7607). It is the structured-output attempt path for ALL SEVEN sections' first-attempt + retry + repair + verifier-repair, and it carries CompetitorLandscape ad-evidence normalization (normalizedAdEvidenceGroups built only for positioningCompetitorLandscape at 7754), the BuyerICP/Competitor/VoC gap-artifact suite (3785-3845), and the full VoC self-sourcing + model-authored-gap block (3838-3868). Scoping the gate's evaluateCommittableAttempt to treat callStructuredAttempt as 'paid-media (url-only loadBearingKinds)' and buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput as 'the 6 answer-tool sections' is the wrong axis entirely — both builders are section-agnostic and the sectionId branch INSIDE each is what differentiates. The gate must key loadBearingKinds + hook off the runtime input.sectionId in BOTH builders, and BOTH builders must thread the SAME per-section hook (VoC self-sourcing fires in callStructuredAttempt too, at 3838). The design's two-line characterization will mis-route every section that takes the structured-retry path.

The postRequiredEvidenceHook seam covers ONLY the escape hatches that sit BETWEEN requiredEvidence and evidenceSupport (VoC self-sourcing, DemandIntent softening). It silently omits the FOUR section-specific gap-artifact builders that fire on the MINIMUMS-FAILED branch (run-section.ts:3785 buildBuyerICPPersonaEvidenceGapArtifact, 3792 buildCompetitorStrategicEvidenceGapArtifact, 5235 buildOfferDiagnosticEvidenceGapArtifact, 3800/5243 buildVoiceOfCustomerAttemptEvidenceGapArtifact). These populate AttemptResult.{buyerICPEvidenceGapArtifact, competitorStrategicEvidenceGapArtifact, offerDiagnosticEvidenceGapArtifact, voiceOfCustomerEvidenceGapArtifact} which downstream orchestration commits as honest-gap drafts (the 32cc78c4 / W1 ungate posture). The verdict union has a 'minimumsFailed' variant with errors:string[] but NO field for these four gap artifacts. If the caller maps minimumsFailed -> errors only, every minimums-failed BuyerICP/Competitor/Offer/VoC section LOSES its gap artifact and stalls the run <7/7 instead of committing-with-gap. The hook seam is in the wrong position to fix this — these are PRE-requiredEvidence, pre-hook.

VoC loadBearingKinds is documented in the design as ['numeric','url','quote'] and confirmed correct (evidence-support.ts:32). But the design's claim that this lives in 'two ad-hoc ternaries' undersells it: there is also a THIRD VoC committability path — buildVoiceOfCustomerDeterministicSynthesisArtifact at run-section.ts:949 (the helper the design mislabels as '@~1010'). That helper runs validateMinimums + checkRequiredEvidenceClasses + checkVoiceOfCustomerSelfSourcing and returns ArtifactEnvelope|undefined with NO evidence-support evaluation, NO RequiredEvidenceMissingError reconstruction, and NO loadBearingKinds. The gate interface has no verdict variant matching 'returns undefined on any failure, no error surfaced' — its narrowest failure variant still carries errors[]. Threading this helper through evaluateCommittableAttempt (design STEP 6 'investigate') is not a tail-cleanup: it is a third live builder with a structurally incompatible return contract (undefined-means-fallback, not throw, not verdict). If it is live, the gate either changes its contract (behavior regression in the VoC deterministic-synthesis fallback) or stays a 3rd un-consolidated copy — defeating the consolidation premise.

The not-probed sentinel is NOT a symmetric 'duplicated string' that can be lifted to one constant cleanly. required-evidence.ts:203 is a REGEX matcher (/not probed this run/i.test(reason)) testing an arbitrary reason field; competitor-ad-adapter.ts:806 EMITS a full sentence ('LinkedIn ad library was not probed this run; LinkedIn counts are structurally 0...'). The coupling is producer-emits-sentence / consumer-regex-matches-substring. A single NOT_PROBED_SENTINEL string constant breaks this: the consumer must keep matching ANY reason containing the phrase (other producers may emit different sentences containing 'not probed this run'), so replacing the regex with === constant equality NARROWS the matcher and could let a differently-worded not-probed reason fall through to a hard RequiredEvidenceMissingError. The 'byte-identical parity test' the design proposes pins the wrong invariant — it pins producer==constant, but the load-bearing invariant is consumer-regex-still-matches-all-not-probed-variants.

LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED ceiling: the design correctly scopes getEvidenceGateFailureReason (1560) OUT, but the gate's evidenceShortfall verdict returns committableArtifact + shortfall and asserts the caller 'can still commit-with-honest-badge.' Verified the orchestrator gate default is Infinity (evidence-support.ts:42 defaultMaxUnsupportedAllowed). The hole: the gate computes unsupportedLoadBearing using the INJECTED per-section loadBearingKinds (VoC=+quote), but getEvidenceGateFailureReason downstream reads getUnsupportedLoadBearingCount(attempt) off the AttemptResult. If the consolidated gate writes a DIFFERENT unsupportedLoadBearing set into AttemptResult than the per-builder ternaries did today (e.g. because the sectionId lookup is applied in a builder that previously used default kinds — like the structured-retry path for VoC which today at callStructuredAttempt:3765 applies ONLY the paid-media ternary and would have used DEFAULT kinds for VoC, NOT voiceOfCustomerLoadBearingKinds), then VoC's unsupported count CHANGES on the retry path, shifting what the Infinity-or-finite ceiling sees. The design assumes loadBearingKinds was already per-section everywhere; it was not — callStructuredAttempt only ever applied the paid-media override, so VoC running through callStructuredAttempt today gets default ['numeric','url'] not +quote. Unifying the lookup is a BEHAVIOR CHANGE for VoC-on-retry, not a pure consolidation.

DemandIntent SpyFu softening: the design's HookOutcome 'soften' variant returns a replacement artifact, and the gate threads it to evidence-support. But the actual code (5408-5417) re-binds committedArtifact and the softened path has its OWN re-validation ('softened === undefined -> fall back to nulling'). The hook contract {kind:'soften', artifact} provides no channel for the soften operation to FAIL and demand a null/hard-fail (it can only return ok/reject/soften). The real code can produce buildDemandIntentSpyFuToolGapArtifact()===undefined and must then hard-fail with a specific error. 'soften' has no failure sub-state, so a failed softening would either be mis-encoded as {kind:'ok'} (commits a still-dirty body — the exact fabrication the softening guards against) or forced into {kind:'reject'} (losing the specific 'did not re-pass validation' error). The HookOutcome union is missing a 'soften-failed' terminal.
orderingProblems: Phase 2 (c1 gate) is ordered BEFORE Phase 3 (c2 provenance) and Phase 4 (c3 descriptor), with c2/c3 both declaring dependsOnPhases including 2. But the gate's central deliverable — the sectionId->loadBearingKinds lookup — is the SAME data c3's SectionDefinition.loadBearingKinds descriptor field is meant to own (design says c3 'folds in the c1 sectionId->kinds lookup'). So c1 builds the lookup as a standalone map, then c3 MOVES it onto the descriptor and re-points the gate at descriptor.loadBearingKinds. That is exactly the backtracking the notes claim to avoid: the gate gets re-touched in Phase 4 to read from the descriptor instead of its own map. Either loadBearingKinds belongs on the descriptor from the start (do a minimal descriptor field in Phase 2) or the gate owns it permanently (drop it from c3). The current split guarantees the gate is edited twice.

Phase 3 (c2 provenance) declares dependsOnPhases:[1] (NOT [1,2]) in the graph, yet the prose says 'c2 specifically benefits from c1 consolidated gate (evidence-support is where the rescue/shortfall logic lives)' and the verifier reads must thread through the gate's evidence-support path. The dependency array and the rationale disagree. If c2 truly depends only on Phase 1, it can land in parallel with c1 — but then c2 modifies structural-verifier/evidence-support while c1 is also restructuring the evaluateEvidenceSupport call sequence, producing a merge collision on the same evidence-support boundary. The graph understates the c1<->c2 coupling; they must be serialized or they race the verifier seam.

Phase 9 (c8+c9) declares dependsOnPhases:[5] (after c4 god-module decomposition) because 'saveArtifact callers live in run-section which c4 relocates.' But c9 (supabase-run-store post-commit extraction) and c8 (realtime broadcaster contract) operate in src/lib/research-v2/supabase-run-store.ts and section-partial-broadcaster.ts — NOT in run-section.ts. The dependency on Phase 5 is asserted via 'saveArtifact callers live in run-section' but saveArtifact is in the run-store, and the broadcaster is called from the section job, not run-section. This is a phantom dependency that needlessly serializes a fully-independent cleanup behind the riskiest phase (c4). c8 at minimum (display contract, no E2E) could run anytime in parallel.

Phase 1 deletes streamRunSection/streamSectionViaAnswerTool/callStructuredStreamAttempt and claims this 'drops the 3 validation copies to 2.' But callStructuredStreamAttempt (the deleted one) is the DEAD copy; the TWO survivors (callStructuredAttempt, buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput) are BOTH live and BOTH section-agnostic with the full sectionId-branching suite — plus the THIRD live copy buildVoiceOfCustomerDeterministicSynthesisArtifact at 949 that the design never counts as a validation copy in Phase 1's accounting. So Phase 1's premise 'gate only has to unify 2 live builders' is wrong: it is 3 (the VoC deterministic synthesis helper is a live committability path). This mis-count propagates into Phase 2's scope and verification gate (which never lists a test for the 949 helper).
missedCallSites: run-section.ts:949 buildVoiceOfCustomerDeterministicSynthesisArtifact — a THIRD live committability path (minimums + requiredEvidence + VoC self-sourcing, returns ArtifactEnvelope|undefined, NO evidence-support, NO error surfacing). Design references it as '@~1010' and only as a STEP-6 'investigate' tail item; it is actually the function STARTING at 949 and is a structurally distinct builder the gate interface cannot represent (undefined-as-fallback contract).

callStructuredAttempt live call sites 7780, 7829, 7901, 8034 (first-attempt, retry, repair, verifier-repair INSIDE runSection at 7607) — the design lists callStructuredAttempt as a single paid-media builder and never enumerates that it is invoked 4x per run for ALL sections through the retry/repair ladder. Each invocation must produce identical gate behavior; the migration STEP 5 'migrate callStructuredAttempt (paid-media live path)' will only test paid-media and miss the BuyerICP/Competitor/VoC retry paths that also flow through it.

The four minimums-failed gap-artifact builders: run-section.ts:3785/3792/3800 (callStructuredAttempt branch) and 5221/5228/5235/5243 (buildVerifiedAttemptFromOutput branch) — buildBuyerICPPersonaEvidenceGapArtifact, buildCompetitorStrategicEvidenceGapArtifact, buildOfferDiagnosticEvidenceGapArtifact, buildVoiceOfCustomerAttemptEvidenceGapArtifact. These populate AttemptResult gap-artifact fields on the MINIMUMS branch, before any hook runs. The CommittableVerdict 'minimumsFailed' variant carries no slot for them, so the caller cannot reconstruct them from the verdict — a silent regression to the W1/32cc78c4 honest-gap commit path that keeps runs at 7/7.

structural-verifier.ts:355 marker-rescue path (the c2 target) hardcodes matchedSourceRef:{kind:'userProvided'} with NO field — confirmed. But ALSO structural-verifier.ts:516 and :551/:569 read matchedSourceRef.kind==='userProvided' for the deterministicVerdict entailment downgrade. c2's plan to thread field+provenance through 'both match paths' omits these THREE additional read sites (516/551/569) that consume the rescued verdict's source ref shape — adding a field to the rescue ref without updating these risks the entailment verdict flipping for legitimately user-asserted claims.

audit-reader-shell.tsx:571 isSixSectionComplete + :1026 consumer, run-lab-section/route.ts:260 isSixSectionRollupComplete + :301 consumer — c5 claims FOUR completeness predicates; only TWO of the four named (isSixSectionRollupComplete, isSixSectionComplete) were confirmable by grep in the searched dirs. The other two (hasSixPositioningSectionsComplete @use-audit-state:35, deriveParentStatus @audit-state/route:374) were not surfaced in this pass — c5's 'route all four through one source of truth' must first VERIFY all four still exist at the cited lines; a stale predicate cited in the plan that no longer exists (or a fifth clone that does) would silently leave a drift path unrouted.

evidence-support.ts:76 getMaxUnsupportedAllowed + run-section.ts:6238/6817/7943/8072 getEvidenceGateFailureReason — the design says these stay untouched and the gate 'just supplies the unsupportedLoadBearing list they already consume.' But because unifying loadBearingKinds CHANGES the unsupportedLoadBearing set for VoC-on-the-structured-retry-path (today default kinds, post-gate +quote), the downstream getEvidenceGateFailureReason at 7943/8072 (the post-repair ceiling gate) will see a different count for VoC even with the ceiling left alone. The 'untouched' claim is true for the code but false for the BEHAVIOR — a regression-test on VoC-with-quote-claims-on-retry under a finite LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED is missing from every verification gate.
